I'm in therapy for my 14-hour-a-day phone addiction and I'm determined to beat it
Addiction treatment centres say more clients are coming for help with "out-of-control" phone use.
Kunal Shah: The Indian entrepreneur taking charge of WhatsApp
Kunal Shah has been a recognisable figure in India's startup ecosystem for a while but now he faces global spotlight.
'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
As autonomous urban delivery vehicles increasingly take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.
I tested AI glasses in Paris. Here's what they got wrong
Wearable AI can help travellers navigate cities, translate menus and fundamentally transform travel. But what are the trade-offs?

AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.
Culture secretary quits X in protest at 'misinformation'
Lisa Nandy says her department is also leaving Elon Musk's platform, which she claims "isn't healthy for our democracy".
Meta glasses wearers hit with paywall to use built-in feature
A feature in Meta's glasses which boosts the voice of people will be capped at three hours for free use.
Data centre planned for Microsoft's UK HQ
A public consultation on the proposal will be held by developers in Sonning next week.
Support floods in for influencer Nara Smith after daughter's cancer diagnosis
In an emotional video, Nara Smith reveals toddler Whimsy Lou was diagnosed with cancer late last year.
Google must pay €4.1bn fine for using Android to 'block' rivals
A Google spokesperson said the judgement "fails to recognise" the firm's "significant investment to ensure Android remains open."
India asks WhatsApp to pause username feature rollout over fraud concerns
WhatsApp says the feature is not yet live and that it has built in safeguards against potential scams and impersonation.
Singapore seizes $42m mansion over Nvidia chip smuggling
The luxury home was seized as part of a probe into the illegal trade of servers containing AI semiconductors.
Clinical trial aims to help heart failure patients
Patients will wear smart rings that can monitor if iron treatments help heart conditions.
How a town is encouraging young people away from smartphones
The campaign launched in Solihull aims to encourage organisations to sign up to be smartphone-free
Don't expect connected car apps to save your stolen car, experts say
Kia told the BBC UK law prevented its location tracking function being used to live track vehicles.
Battery disposal warning after bin lorry fire
The fire was caused by a lithium battery incorrectly disposed in household waste, the council says.
Wonka Netflix show faces backlash for AI-generated Gene Wilder voice
The actor, who died in 2016, appears in the Netflix show with the consent of his estate.
'Delivery robots are in the way - you're the one who has to move'
People in Leeds have their say about delivery robots as a charity questions the devices' legality.
Australia sues Amazon for making allegedly unfair contracts with subscribers
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) says Amazon has broken consumer protection law

IN CASE YOU MISSED IT
I'm in therapy for my 14-hour-a-day phone addiction and I'm determined to beat it
Addiction treatment centres say more clients are coming for help with "out-of-control" phone use.
Kunal Shah: The Indian entrepreneur taking charge of WhatsApp
Kunal Shah has been a recognisable figure in India's startup ecosystem for a while but now he faces global spotlight.
'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
As autonomous urban delivery vehicles increasingly take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.
I tested AI glasses in Paris. Here's what they got wrong
Wearable AI can help travellers navigate cities, translate menus and fundamentally transform travel. But what are the trade-offs?

MORE 6 hrs ago AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence? Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system. 9 hrs ago Culture secretary quits X in protest at 'misinformation' Lisa Nandy says her department is also leaving Elon Musk's platform, which she claims "isn't healthy for our democracy". 16 hrs ago Meta glasses wearers hit with paywall to use built-in feature A feature in Meta's glasses which boosts the voice of people will be capped at three hour
AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.
Culture secretary quits X in protest at 'misinformation'
Lisa Nandy says her department is also leaving Elon Musk's platform, which she claims "isn't healthy for our democracy".
Meta glasses wearers hit with paywall to use built-in feature
A feature in Meta's glasses which boosts the voice of people will be capped at three hours for free use.
Data centre planned for Microsoft's UK HQ
A public consultation on the proposal will be held by developers in Sonning next week.
Support floods in for influencer Nara Smith after daughter's cancer diagnosis
In an emotional video, Nara Smith reveals toddler Whimsy Lou was diagnosed with cancer late last year.
Google must pay €4.1bn fine for using Android to 'block' rivals
A Google spokesperson said the judgement "fails to recognise" the firm's "significant investment to ensure Android remains open."
India asks WhatsApp to pause username feature rollout over fraud concerns
WhatsApp says the feature is not yet live and that it has built in safeguards against potential scams and impersonation.
Singapore seizes $42m mansion over Nvidia chip smuggling
The luxury home was seized as part of a probe into the illegal trade of servers containing AI semiconductors.
Clinical trial aims to help heart failure patients
Patients will wear smart rings that can monitor if iron treatments help heart conditions.
How a town is encouraging young people away from smartphones
The campaign launched in Solihull aims to encourage organisations to sign up to be smartphone-free
Don't expect connected car apps to save your stolen car, experts say
Kia told the BBC UK law prevented its location tracking function being used to live track vehicles.
Battery disposal warning after bin lorry fire
The fire was caused by a lithium battery incorrectly disposed in household waste, the council says.
Wonka Netflix show faces backlash for AI-generated Gene Wilder voice
The actor, who died in 2016, appears in the Netflix show with the consent of his estate.
'Delivery robots are in the way - you're the one who has to move'
People in Leeds have their say about delivery robots as a charity questions the devices' legality.
Australia sues Amazon for making allegedly unfair contracts with subscribers
The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) says Amazon has broken consumer protection law
